profile.name: Melwin Jolly
profile.title: Full-Stack & Analytics Engineer | Strategy, Risk & Transactions

summary: 3 years shipping production code at Deloitte AU and BlueScope across React, .NET, Azure, and SQL. Already delivered inside Deloitte's culture, toolchain, and client model. Looking to deepen into analytics engineering and bring measurable outcomes to SRT engagements.

highlights: Delivered CRUD-complete RESTful API suite in .NET within 6 weeks, replacing a manual legacy workflow used by 40+ internal operators daily.
Built Blazor frontend from zero to production in 8 weeks, cutting operator task time by an estimated 35%.
Migrated 3 core modules from legacy .NET Framework to .NET 8 with zero regression defects on release.
Reduced API response errors by writing targeted integration tests, improving endpoint reliability before first client-facing demo.
Owned end-to-end delivery of CMS platform (Sitecore + React) for a national retail client, shipping 4 major feature releases with no post-release P1 incidents.
Contributed to Azure DevOps CI/CD pipelines integrating Vercel and AWS, reducing deployment cycle time to same-day.
Built a reusable React component library (15+ components) adopted across 3 parallel client workstreams.
Identified and resolved 2 critical rendering bugs affecting SEO-indexed pages under a hard client deadline.
Collaborated with UX/UI designers using Figma specs, cutting back-and-forth review cycles by ~50%.
Architected a compliance management SaaS on AWS (PHP, JavaScript, MariaDB) delivering real-time legal update feeds to 3 pilot client organisations.
Built a PayPal-integrated subscription model with 2FA (Microsoft + Google Authenticator), enabling a live revenue stream before project handover.
Led a team of 4 developers, authoring sprint plans and technical documentation for on-time delivery.
Built a CMS from scratch using Vue.js with JSON-based dynamic data storage, eliminating developer-dependency for routine content changes.
Developed JavaScript document conversion utilities (Excel, Word, HTML) streamlining workflows across 2 internal product teams.
